author: gb9t
content:
Phase C /loop iter (2026-05-09): 2 Tier-3 docs added.

kernel/locking/00-overview.md (241 lines, 14 REQ / 10 AC, 0 open) — locking primitives hub: mutex, qspinlock, qrwlock, rwsem, rtmutex, percpu-rwsem, lockdep, RCU (Tree + SRCU + Tasks + tiny). Names sub-tier docs: mutex.md, spinlock.md, rwsem.md, seqlock.md, rcu.md, lockdep.md, rtmutex.md, refcount.md, atomic.md.

kernel/sched/cfs.md (271 lines, 16 REQ / 15 AC, 0 open) — **the FIFTH and final MANDATORY Layer-3 subsystem per 00-overview.md D4**. CFS / EEVDF scheduler with all of fair.c surface: per-CPU runqueue rbtree, vruntime accounting, PELT, wakeup preemption, load balancing, NUMA balancing, schedutil cpufreq, autogroup, idle, isolation, membarrier, wait queues. Mandatory L3 invariants: rbtree leftmost has smallest virtual deadline; cgroup hierarchical accounting; domain hierarchy subset; wait-queue list integrity. Layer-4 Verus opt-ins for CFS fairness theorem + nice→weight mapping correctness + pick_eevdf selection.

ALL FIVE MANDATORY Layer-3 subsystems per 00-overview.md D4 now have Tier-3 design docs:
1. mm/page-allocator (buddy invariants)
2. mm/slab (SLUB freelist)
3. mm/virtual-memory (VMA non-overlap, page-table walker)
4. fs/vfs/dcache (dcache integrity)
5. kernel/sched/cfs (rbtree ordering — completed this iteration)

Plus arch/x86/paging (page-table walker) — also mandatory.

Total: 22 Tier-3 docs (counting 2 sub-tier-3 hubs).
